evidence_quotes.quote: EULA's which give publishers the ability to retract a game
"Termination" the legal owner is demanded to both uninstall the game digitally and destroy the physical copy
rights of ownership on the very things we pay to own be taken away from European citizen.

paragraphs.text: Greetings and well regards to anyone who reads this. I am a EU Citizen who cares a lot about the games industry, games as a creative form of expression and games as a form of way to connect with other people. In recent years games have been changed to include lines in the EULA's which give publishers the ability to retract a game which has been purchased fairly and legally by consumers on as little of a basis as the company in question simply wished to have it removed from the hands of the one who bought it. (As an example I am attaching the End User Licence Agreement from Ubisoft typical to their games, where in their 8th section deem that for any reason upon what they declare "Termination" the legal owner is demanded to both uninstall the game digitally and destroy the physical copy) If I purchased a washing machine isn't it reasonable to say that I should be allowed to keep it til the moment I find it pertinent to remove it myself? Or should the company who built and produced it be allowed to take something I legally bought away from me merely on the grounds that I can't purchase it unless I agree to give the company the means of taking it away should they themselves not want to let me use it anymore? As a EU Citizen I would be proud to see our Digital Fairness Act include video games in it as to prevent the industry to go down a path that may result in business practices that will lead to lower faith in a leading industry for entertainment that has a strong foothold in several european countries export of entertainment but also in rights of ownership on the very things we pay to own be taken away from European citizen. So that's why I wish to leave the suggestion here that the Digital Fairness Act should both include Video Games as a topic which it should protect but also add that it will ensure ownership of said entertainment in the hands of those who purchased them.
